text:
Estelle Allen is straight-up redefining what it means to mix hyperpop and hyper-rock vibes. With raw emotion, chaotic energy, and lyrics that hit like a sucker punch, she’s the underground phenom your playlists desperately need. She’s got a sound that’s messy in the best way—like your emotions after a 3 a.m. spiral. Let’s break down the madness, one banger at a time.

\- DUI: This track is reckless AF, and not just because of the lyrics. The chaotic energy of "you should drive drunk" feels like a satirical gut-punch to modern nihilism. The verses hit hard with absurd justifications for bad decisions, blending humor and darkness like a drunk night out. It’s a bold commentary on self-destructive behavior. listen here 🚗💥😵💫
\=> <https://open.spotify.com/track/3RLI8S7KpEZs4SqePGjM2R>

\- Girlfriend: If yearning had a soundtrack, this would be it. Estelle spills her heart out with "I think I wanna be your girlfriend" while blending quirky gaming references like fighting Artorias or reading Homestuck. It’s geeky, tender, and a little manic—basically, the feeling of falling for someone who makes you lose your mind in the best way. listen here 💖🌸🎮
\=> <https://open.spotify.com/track/1J2Vj3lDjNE9e8G33qDZ5Q>

\- Sober: This one is a full-on existential crisis. Estelle’s lyrics dive into the messiness of sobriety, boredom, and the pull of old habits. The verse "take me back to the menagerie, my god I miss it" is haunting, reflecting the struggle to grow while clinging to chaos. The production has a woozy vibe that mirrors the content perfectly. listen here 🤯🥀🍷
\=> <https://open.spotify.com/track/2yjBg1CPPVJreXCzBwM3Dn>

\- Chrysalis: This track is a transformation anthem wrapped in chaos. "I just hopped out the chrysalis, give me something to put on" screams rebirth while the messy, aggressive beat mirrors the upheaval of becoming something new. It’s equal parts empowering and disorienting, with surreal lyrics about breaking free from the past. listen here 🦋🔥⚡
\=> <https://open.spotify.com/track/1tpac31GG4przsH6DrhHWM>

\- Gum: This is peak meltdown energy. Estelle captures the surreal mix of nausea and emotional overload with "do you have any gum? 'Cause I just threw up." The track builds with chaotic layers of sound, ending in a cathartic scream-fest that feels like letting out all your bottled-up emotions. listen here 💥🤮🎤
\=> <https://open.spotify.com/track/6nU9sd3xQFJG5Jc3LPpLir>

Estelle Allen’s sound is all about raw vulnerability and catharsis. She’s not afraid to wade into the messiest parts of being human, and her tracks hit with a mix of humor, heartbreak, and chaos. If you’re into hyperpop that pushes boundaries and dives headfirst into emotional chaos, she’s your next obsession. 💅🎶🔥
